hypotheses.label: grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural splice/recurse mutators place MORE DISTINCT coherent structural tokens/constructs per seed than cmplog's I2S byte-substitution can, even though cmplog can place individual literals

registry_tool_repointed_arms.note: joint_necessity GENERIC 2-arm analyze_pair (--winner-fuzzer grimoire --loser-fuzzer cmplog). The decisive pair for this shape is grimoire>cmplog, so the blocking arm is CMPLOG, not naive. analyze_pair emits canonical winner_tags/loser_tags/tag_lift/size_lift plus armtag aliases grimoire_tags/cmplog_tags. tag_lift is the DIFFERENCE (grimoire_tags - cmplog_tags) of mean distinct-token counts; size_lift is the median-size RATIO grimoire/cmplog.

params.tokens_note: harfbuzz tokens are the 4-byte sfnt table tags + sfnt/ttc magic ('true','ttcf','OTTO') the GeneralizationStage gaps-and-preserves as coherent runs. The per-target token vocab is a measurement PARAM, not a new descriptor.

hypotheses.prediction: grimoire_tags - cmplog_tags >= 1.0: a grimoire seed carries on average at least one MORE distinct sfnt-structural token than a cmplog blocking seed. Rules out BYTE-RICHNESS-NOT-STRUCTURE: cmplog runs I2S, so if the advantage were merely placing magic/tag LITERALS (a byte capability cmplog ALSO has via input-to-state), cmplog_tags would match grimoire_tags and tag_lift -> 0. A positive tag_lift against the I2S arm isolates grammar-aware multi-token RECOMBINATION from literal substitution.
hypotheses.rule_explanation: Unchanged from round 1/2. Confirmed harfbuzz/5311 (tag_lift 3.13, grimoire_tags 3.33, size_lift 24.0) and harfbuzz/6181 (tag_lift 4.6, grimoire_tags 5.5, size_lift 7.83).
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: Could come out the other way: cmplog's I2S can substitute the 4-byte table-tag literals directly; if the gate were cleared by placing the tag LITERAL rather than recombining a coherent multi-table structure, cmplog_tags would equal/exceed grimoire_tags and tag_lift -> 0, refuting the hypothesis.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Label SOURCE is the resolve pattern (grimoire resolves / cmplog blocks). Verification SIGNAL is the distinct-structural-token count + median size of the seed BYTES, never re-reading whether the branch flipped.

hypotheses.label: fallback: grimoire reaches the gate via larger/deeper structure (state-machine depth, repeated category blocks, nested clusters) without a countable target-vocabulary tag signal

registry_tool_repointed_arms.note: Same grimoire-vs-cmplog repoint; consumes only the size_lift metric.

hypotheses.prediction: size_lift >= 1.3 (grimoire seeds materially larger/deeper) even when tag_lift is ~0, because the gate is about state-machine DEPTH / repeated category structure rather than a countable table tag.
hypotheses.rule_explanation: Unchanged from round 1/2. Consulted only when structural_token_richness does NOT fire. Confirmed harfbuzz/5958 (size_lift 4.21) and libxml2/6743 (size_lift 2.22).
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: If grimoire and cmplog seeds are the same size, size_lift -> ~1.0 and this is refuted.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Median seed size is a byte-length property of the corpus, independent of the resolve label.

hypotheses.label: grimoire's grammar mutators place structural tokens/constructs into the corpus that naive's byte havoc cannot, building larger/deeper inputs

registry_tool_repointed_arms.note: Repoint resolving_arm=grimoire, blocking_arm=naive (a param). tag_lift = grimoire_tags vs naive_tags; size_lift = median grimoire seed size vs median naive seed size; naive_tags = structural-token count in the naive (blocking) seed set.

params.tokens_note: harfbuzz tokens are the 4-byte sfnt table tags + magic; libxml2 tokens are XML/DTD structural constructs grimoire splices that naive byte havoc does not. The per-target token vocab is a measurement param; the descriptor is target-agnostic.

hypotheses.prediction: naive_tags ~ 0 (byte havoc places essentially no coherent structural tokens) AND grimoire_tags >> naive_tags AND size_lift >= 1.0. A pure-byte-mutation alternative predicts naive_tags ~ grimoire_tags and tag_lift ~ 0.
hypotheses.rule: tag_lift >= 1.0 AND naive_tags < grimoire_tags AND grimoire_tags >= 2
hypotheses.rule_explanation: Unchanged from round 1/2. Confirmed 7 libxml2 branches (6924/6925/6926/6948/6950/7147/7327).
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: If naive's havoc-derived corpus carried as many constructs as grimoire's, tag_lift -> 0 and the hypothesis is refuted.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Measures the STRUCTURAL CONTENT of the seed corpora (token count + size), never re-reads the resolve/block outcome.

hypotheses.label: round 2: grimoire's structural recombination drives the gated branch LINE deeper -- it builds the well-nested element/state-machine structure that advances the shaper/parser INTO the gated case at high per-seed execution count, while cmplog's byte-corrupted input reaches the line far fewer times

registry_tool_repointed_arms.note: depth_reach replays grimoire-resolving (W) vs cmplog-blocking (L) seeds through libafl-harfbuzz-cov and reads the branch line's per-seed-normalized execution count.

hypotheses.prediction: winner_depth substantially exceeds loser_depth, depth_lift >= 1.5 with winner_deeper=true.
hypotheses.rule_explanation: Carried VERBATIM from round 2 for the monotonic-superset invariant. NOTE (round-3 reconciliation): the round-2 arbitration (assignments_s4.json) SCORED this rule on its 3 target branches harfbuzz/5440, /5810, /5866 and it did NOT hold on any of them (all three returned rule_not_met). It therefore has ZERO confirmed branches. It is retained so the superset is strictly monotonic, but it labels nothing; round 3 does NOT re-propose it for those branches (that would be a forbidden relaxed-threshold re-test of an already-scored mechanism).
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: If cmplog's corpus reaches the gated case line as often as grimoire's (depth_lift ~ 1, winner_deeper=false), the block is a downstream value check, not a structural-reach difference -- refuting this mechanism. (This is what the round-2 data showed.)
hypotheses.g2_independence: Signal is the per-seed-normalized EXECUTION COUNT of the branch line when each corpus is replayed through the cov binary, never a re-read of the resolve/block flag.

libxml2_configuration_specific.reason: These libxml2 branches DO have grimoire/cmplog/naive seeds on sB (the round-2 'no corpus' claim was wrong -- assignments_sB.json proves all 36 were scored, 8 validated, these 28 rule_not_met). Their failure is NOT a missing-corpus artifact; it is that their gate mechanism is a SPECIFIC RARE STRUCTURAL CONFIGURATION, not a corpus-statistic. The gates are degenerate-field / node-type / reader-state / hash-collision / NULL-table conditions (namelen<=0, val[0]==0, table[key].valid==0, nbAttrs>=maxAttrs, ref==NULL, reader->node->type==XML_ELEMENT_NODE, dtd->last==NULL, case XML_ELEMENT_TYPE_UNDEFINED, ...). The discriminating feature is the PRESENCE of one particular arrangement in the seed, not 'more tags' or 'a bigger seed' or 'a closer byte to a literal'. Every built corpus-statistic tool measures the wrong axis: joint_necessity (token_count + struct_size_ratio) ALREADY scored all three of its rules on these branches and none held; value_distance_reached is a vp-Hamming-gradient tool and most operands here are none_structural / derived_integer (no fixed byte literal to climb toward), and grimoire is not a gradient mutator; operand_enrichment/byte_freq_ratio is forbidden by data_realities on structural-assembly gates with different-sized seeds; corpus_size_ratio + token_count are the SAME axes joint_necessity already covers (re-testing them = forbidden relaxed re-test). No built tool detects 'this seed contains an empty-name attribute' or 'the reader landed on an element node with a following sibling'. Configuration-specificity has no built measurement, so these are an HONEST decidable:false (correct outcome for a non-local grimoire family), carried to fallback:inconclusive. A discriminating test would need a NEW per-branch structural-predicate extractor (parse the seed, assert the specific gated configuration) -- out of scope for the built-tool registry and itself near-tautological with the gate.

harfbuzz_repeated_motif_state_machine.reason: These three were the round-2 NEW depth_reach target. depth_reach was SCORED on all three (assignments_s4.json, rule_not_met) and did NOT hold -- so depth_reach is now a tried-and-refuted mechanism here and may not be relaxed-re-tested. Their actual mechanism (from the cards) is grimoire assembling a REPETITIVE / SELF-SIMILAR category-byte block -- the repeated 'f2 a8 00 00' dword that drives Indic state-machine case 19 (5810) / indic_symbol_cluster (5866), and the gapped-and-spliced 'true'/'loca' magic-run that keeps the glyf parse valid (5440). The only genuinely-NEW discriminating axis is byte-periodicity / motif self-similarity, for which NO built tool exists (byte_freq_ratio measures fixed-offset target-vs-decoy presence, not periodicity; token_count counts a fixed vocabulary; value_distance measures Hamming-to-literal; depth_reach measures line-execution and already failed). Proposing a new periodicity descriptor is also not robust: 5810 and 5866 carry only n=2 grimoire winner seeds (per round-2's own seed audit), so any new per-seed statistic rests on 2 inputs after depth_reach already failed on these branches. Per G3 -- non-local grimoire families are legitimately non-discriminable -- the honest call is decidable:false rather than inventing a periodicity tool to force a label on 2 seeds. Carried to fallback:inconclusive.

data_realities_respected: joint_necessity (token_count + struct_size_ratio) replays seed BYTES, not mutation_op/lineage, so 'I2S not tagged' and 'value_profile is a feedback' do not constrain it.

CORRECTION of round 2: libxml2 IS scorable on sB -- assignments_sB.json shows all 36 libxml2 branches were arbitrated (8 validated, 28 rule_not_met). The round-2 'libxml2 has no grimoire corpus / decidable:false on a data-gap basis' claim was FALSE. The 28 residual libxml2 branches are decidable:false for the CORRECT reason (configuration-specific mechanism with no built measurement), not a missing-corpus artifact.

structural-assembly gates have different-SIZED winning vs blocking seeds, so byte-alignment (byte_freq_ratio / operand_enrichment) is NOT proposed for this shape; struct_size_ratio / token_count / depth_reach are the corpus-statistic axes used, and all have now been exhausted on the residual branches.

value_distance_reached is NOT proposed: it is a vp-gradient (Hamming-to-literal) tool; grimoire is a structural mutator, not a gradient feedback, and most residual operands are none_structural / derived_integer with no fixed byte literal.

monotonic_superset_restorations.reason: Restored in round 2; carried unchanged in round 3. Validated 8 branches across sB (7 token_assembly + provides the naive-arm contrast).

Round-2 NEW depth_reach hypothesis. Carried VERBATIM in round 3 for strict monotonic-superset, even though it confirmed ZERO branches (rule_not_met on all 3 targets). It labels nothing; retained only so the rule set is a superset of round 2.

notes: ROUND-3 (FINAL, loop-until-dry) re-design of grimoire_structural_WL. PRIORS KEPT VERBATIM (monotonic superset): all 4 round-2 hypotheses are retained unchanged with their exact rules -- structural_token_richness, structural_size_depth_only, structural_token_assembly (these three together confirmed the 11 frozen branches: 2 token_richness [hb 5311,6181] + 2 size_depth [hb 5958, xml 6743] + 7 token_assembly [xml 6924,6925,6926,6948,6950,7147,7327]) and structural_iteration_depth_reach (carried for superset only -- it confirmed nothing). NO NEW DISCRIMINATING HYPOTHESIS is added, after auditing every remaining built tool against the 31 unlabeled branches: all are honestly decidable:false. RECONCILIATION (key round-3 correction): round 2 wrongly declared the 36 libxml2 branches decidable:false on a 'no grimoire corpus' basis; the actual arbitration (assignments_sB.json) scored all 36 (8 validated, 28 rule_not_met) -- the corpus EXISTS. So this round splits the residual honestly by ROOT CAUSE. (A) 28 libxml2 branches: mechanism is a SPECIFIC RARE STRUCTURAL CONFIGURATION (degenerate empty field, particular node-type/reader-state, hash collision, NULL element table, undefined element-type case). joint_necessity's token+size rules already scored and missed them because the discriminating feature is configuration PRESENCE, not token-count or size; value_distance (vp-gradient) and byte_freq (forbidden on different-sized structural seeds) measure the wrong axis; corpus_size/token_count are the already-exhausted axes. No built tool measures configuration-specificity -> honest decidable:false. (B) 3 harfbuzz branches (5440/5810/5866): the round-2 NEW depth_reach was scored on exactly these and FAILED (rule_not_met) -- so depth_reach is tried-and-refuted and cannot be relaxed-re-tested. Their real mechanism is a REPEATED/SELF-SIMILAR category-byte motif (f2 a8 00 00 dword driving Indic state cases; gapped 'true'/'loca' magic for the glyf gate); the only new axis is byte-periodicity, which has NO built tool AND would rest on n=2 grimoire seeds -- so forcing a label is unjustified. Per G3 (non-local grimoire is legitimately non-discriminable), both groups carry to fallback:inconclusive as HONEST decidable:false. SUMMARY: priors kept = 4 (all verbatim, incl. the 0-confirm depth_reach carried for superset); new mechanism = 0; decidable:false = 31 (28 libxml2 configuration-specific + 3 harfbuzz repeated-motif). This shape is now DRY on the built-tool registry: the 11 measurable structural-richness/size branches are labeled, and the 31 residual branches are an honest non-discriminable gap that would require a NEW structural-predicate / byte-periodicity extractor (out of scope), not a relaxed-threshold re-run of tools already shown to miss them.
